description: The Cataldo 'ndrina is a clan of the 'Ndrangheta based in Locri, Calabria, Italy, founded in the 1960s. It has been involved in a long blood feud with the Cordì 'ndrina since the late 1960s, with periods of truce and resumed hostilities. The clan is engaged in drug trafficking, including cocaine from Colombia, and has been allied with other 'Ndrangheta clans. It remains active as of the 2010s.

country_links.evidence_quote: The Cataldo clan was also involved in cocaine trafficking from Colombia with the Sicilian Cuntrera-Caruana Mafia clan.
country_links.context: The Cataldo clan was involved in cocaine trafficking from Colombia.

country_links.evidence_quote: Territory: Locri , affiliates of the clan in northern Italy
country_links.context: The clan has affiliates in northern Italy.

country_links.evidence_quote: The Sergi-Marando clan imported cocaine from Colombia and hashish from Morocco, while the Cataldos distributed the drugs to Lombardy, Piemonte, Veneto and Emilia Romagna through affiliates of the clan in northern Italy.
country_links.context: The Sergi-Marando clan, associated with the Cataldos, imported hashish from Morocco.
